creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: Stop Making Models Bigger, Make Them Behave — Kobie Crawdord, Snorkel
videos.summary: Kobie Crawford argues that smaller models trained with reinforcement learning to use tools correctly and self-correct outperform much larger models that hallucinate. A 4B Snorkel-finetuned model trained with under $500 solved a SQL task that Qwen 3 235B failed, and single-table training transferred to multi-table tasks with significant benchmark gains.

videos.title: Sovereign Escape Velocity: Ownership w Open Models — Gus Martins, & Ian Ballantyne, Google DeepMind
videos.summary: Gemma 4's 31B model achieves competitive quality on the LM Arena leaderboard while running on a single GPU, compared to competitors requiring four to five GPUs. Variants include 2B and 4B parameter versions optimized for mobile and edge deployment, and the license shifted from custom Gemma to Apache 2.0 to enable sovereign adoption across institutions in emerging markets.

videos.title: Self Driving Products: Product Signals to Pull Requests — Joshua Snyder, PostHog
videos.summary: PostHog is building an autonomous agent pipeline that converts product signals like errors, rage clicks, and customer messages into pull requests. The system groups related signals using LLM-generated query embeddings, researches codebases, and opens PRs automatically.

videos.title: GPU Cloud Deployment Without Leaving Your IDE — Audry Hsu, RunPod
videos.summary: Flash is a decorator-based framework that enables single-command GPU cloud deployment from an IDE with hot reload capabilities. It replaces multi-step Docker and registry workflows with @flash.endpoint annotation on async Python functions, supporting model chaining and serverless autoscaling on RunPod infrastructure.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Google's Agents CLI: The CLI + Skills Combination to Ship AI Agents EASILY
videos.summary: Google's Agents CLI is an open-source tool that enables developers to build and deploy AI agents to production quickly. It provides agent identity, sandboxed code execution, and audit trails for production-grade AI agents. The CLI works with various AI coding assistants including Claude.

videos.title: Live AI Q&A + Crushing it in Chess at the Same Time - Come Hang Out!
videos.summary: Cole Medin is hosting a live AI Q&A session on Saturday where viewers can ask questions about AI coding, agents, Claude Code, and Archon. The stream will also feature chess gameplay alongside the discussion, with content released on a regular weekly schedule.

videos.title: Claude Plans, Gemini Designs: The Workflow to Build BEAUTIFUL Frontends
videos.summary: Cole Medin presents a multi-model workflow that routes planning and reasoning tasks to Claude Opus while delegating frontend design to Gemini 3.5 Flash. The approach leverages each model's strengths: Opus for strategic planning and integration logic, Gemini for visual design and code generation, reducing costs while improving output quality for full-stack applications.

videos.title: Claude Plans, Gemini Designs: One Workflow for Beautiful Frontends (LIVE)
videos.summary: Archon is a workflow framework that combines Claude (Opus) for planning and integration, and Gemini 3.5 Flash for UI design to automate beautiful frontend development. The workflow chains planning, design, integration, and deployment steps across multiple AI models.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: Video explains how to build proactive AI agents and self-improving company systems using AI loops. Covers practical use cases like autonomous ads optimization and SEO, mentioning Loopany, memory, and tools that enable this approach.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses the /goal feature and autonomous coding tools, comparing developments to Ralph-loop 2.0. The video covers practical setup and prompting techniques for goal-driven coding assistance and long-form task exploration through /Mission.

videos.title: New AI coding paradiagm - OpenAI Symphony
videos.summary: OpenAI Symphony is presented as a new AI coding paradigm that integrates automated testing, harness setup, and workflow management. The video demonstrates Symphony's workflow capabilities including setup procedures and integration with AI-driven development practices.

videos.title: Okay, this unleashed my agent
videos.summary: AI Jason reviews free and commercial agent systems including Autoresearch, AutoAgent, and Claude Code. The video demonstrates a 3-layer memory system, self-evolving mechanisms in Hermes, and skill comparison in Openclaw. The creator expresses enthusiasm about the capabilities these agent approaches unlock.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released the Nemotron 3 series consisting of Nano, Super, and Ultra models. The models combine hybrid Mamba transformer architecture with Latent Mixture of Experts and MTP to differentiate from other open models.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax released the M3 language model featuring MiniMax Sparse Attention, which replaces full attention with sparse attention patterns. The model includes tiled I/O improvements and optimized KV cache reading to reduce latency in both prefill and decode stages of inference.

videos.title: Pi Agent explained in 6min..
videos.summary: A video explainer covering Pi, an agent framework that enables different use cases like OpenClaw. The video compares Pi against frontier agent tools like Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and Antigravity, and discusses how the harness layer complexity affects these products.

videos.title: Huawei makes 1.4nm chip??
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code discusses Huawei's announcement of the Tau Scaling Law as a competitive move in semiconductor development. The video covers EUV/DUV lithography, packaging, and logic folding as key industry focus areas in the ongoing hardware race between China and the USA.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: AI and the Job Market in 2026
videos.summary: AWS Developers discusses how AI is reshaping tech hiring and job hunting in 2026. The video covers networking strategies, personal branding, and argues that junior developers remain valuable despite AI automation, using historical job market evolution as precedent.

videos.title: 5 Tips for Deploying AI Agents to Production
videos.summary: AWS video outlines five production best practices for deploying TypeScript AI agents: streaming tool call events for responsiveness, layered authentication with API Gateway and Cognito, type-safe contracted database access with tenant isolation, guardrails and cost budgets via lifecycle hooks and model routing, and observability through metrics and OpenTelemetry tracing. The patterns apply across platforms despite AWS-specific tooling examples.

videos.title: Infrastructure as Code for Beginners (AWS CDK)
videos.summary: A tutorial on Infrastructure as Code using AWS CDK with Python to build a serverless REST API. The video contrasts manual AWS console setup (ClickOps) with automated infrastructure definition using CDK, covering DynamoDB, Lambda, and API Gateway deployment.

videos.title: Stop AI Hallucinations With These 5 Techniques
videos.summary: AWS Developers demonstrates five techniques to reduce hallucinations in AI agents: semantic tool selection to filter context, graph RAG for precise queries, multi-agent validation to catch failures, symbolic guardrails via Python code rules, and runtime guardrails for agent self-correction. The video shows token usage improvements and working examples using a travel agent built with Strands Agents.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: Alberta Tech compares OpenAI Codex and Claude for code generation capabilities and examines whether Google engineers use such tools. The video includes projections of Google's code generation practices through 2026.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: Alberta Tech discusses Malus, a company or project, and its perceived impact on open source development. The video examines how Malus operates and raises questions about its role as a potential threat to open source maintainers and the future of the ecosystem.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech conducted an experiment letting Claude Code handle coding work and found it performed better than manual work. The video explores whether AI coding assistants can fully replace human developers and covers the tool's capabilities and limitations across a workflow.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, argues that rabid fans are not proof of real product-market fit and that true PMF manifests as customer pull rather than founder push. He advocates skipping formal customer interviews in favor of embedded relationships and warns against confusing smart bets on AI model trajectories with convenient delusions.

videos.title: He Raised $26M Betting on Probabilities, Not Guesses | Koah Labs, Nic Baird
videos.summary: Nic Baird, founder of Koah Labs, argues that ad-based monetization is the better bet for AI tools over subscriptions and that human trust adoption, not technology, is the current bottleneck for AI agents. He discusses decision-making frameworks from competitive sailing applied to startup building and fundraising.

videos.title: I Hit $1M ARR in 117 Days. Bootstrapped to $10M. Here's My Playbook | Chatbase, Yasser Elsaid
videos.summary: Yasser Elsaid, founder of Chatbase, shares his bootstrapping strategy that achieved $1M ARR in 117 days and scaled to $10M without external funding. The discussion covers key business decisions including product-led growth, SEO strategy, pricing experiments, churn reduction, and when to raise capital.

videos.title: A CS Professor on Why Slow Learning Wins in the AI Era | CU Boulder, Tom Yeh
videos.summary: Tom Yeh argues that teaching AI by hand at human speed, focusing on mathematical foundations rather than latest tools, creates resilient skills that survive hype cycles. He contends that people must change AI rather than AI changing people, rejecting the "AI-native" trap in education.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: Anthropic's Brand New AI Just Started the Gold Rush (Fable 5)
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Anthropic's new Claude Fable 5 model and its applications in regulated industries. The video covers new use cases for the model and introduces "Know Your Agent" methodology for effective context engineering in long-running AI applications.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
